title: celtis ehrenbergiana var. discolor
intro: Celtis ehrenbergiana var. discolor, also known as the Desert Hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the deserts of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant's health benefits include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from fever and pain. The Desert Hackberry can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
